videos.title: Portugal Art Retreat Printed Goodies!
videos.description:
Retreat prep is in full swing ✨

I made custom booklets, art prints, and stickers for attendees… but if you’re coming next week, look away now 🙈

I can’t wait for tue adventure to begin! Stay tuned, I’ll be sharing more behind the scenes on my Instagram all week! 🎨

https://www.instagram.com/lisabardot

videos.title: What I Use When I’m Not Drawing on My iPad
videos.description:
I’m sharing my favorite analog art supplies, from sketchbooks and ink pens to watercolor, gouache, and colored pencils. If you love drawing or want to explore beyond digital art, these are the tools I keep coming back to.

You probably know me for Procreate tutorials and news, but today I’m setting aside the iPad.

Analog art is a huge part of how I stay inspired. I love drawing in sketchbooks, trying different mediums, and making art with tools that feel tactile, playful, and a little unpredict

videos.title: Analog Meets Digital: Fun Mixed Media Illustrations in Procreate // New Class!
videos.description:
Take the full class in Art Maker's Club: https://www.artmakersclub.com/analog-meets-digital
Or take it on Skillshare: https://skl.sh/4m9NBbz

If your digital art has ever felt a little too clean or a little too perfect, this is a different way to work.

In Analog Meets Digital, you’ll combine real-life materials like paper, paint, and textured pieces with Procreate to create illustrations that feel more expressive and full of character.

Inside the class, we’ll go step by step through the proces

videos.title: How I Make Digital Collage in Procreate  #procreateart #procreate  #digitalart
videos.description:
Take a peek into my process of making digital collages in Procreate. It's actually quite easy thanks to the iOS "Lift Subject" feature. From the Photos or Preview app, long press on a subject in a photo to lift it from the background, then you can drag and drop it right into Procreate! Be warned, it doesn't always work, so you may have to manually cut out some subjects right in Procreate using the selection tool.

You can find lots of free-to-use stock photos from sites like:
https://unsplash.co

videos.title: iPad Air vs iPad Pro Screen Brightness #digitalart #procreate
videos.description:
A quick comparison of screen brightness on the iPad Air vs iPad Pro.

The iPad Pro has a brighter display, which can make a noticeable difference when working outdoors, viewing artwork, or watching video. In this short clip, I show the two side by side so you can see the difference for yourself.

If you’re deciding which iPad to buy for Procreate or digital art, display quality is one of the factors worth considering.

A full video about the best iPad for Procreate is on my channel now!
Watch ➡

videos.title: What is the Best iPad for Procreate in 2026? - First Look at iPad Air M4
videos.description:
If you're trying to decide which iPad to buy for Procreate, this video will help you figure it out.

Apple now has several iPads that work well for drawing, including the newly released iPad Air M4, iPad Pro, and standard iPad, but the best choice depends on how you plan to use Procreate.

In this video I break down the differences between the current iPads and explain which ones make the most sense for artists. We'll talk about performance, Apple Pencil compatibility, screen size, storage, and

videos.title: ASMR iPad Air M4 Unboxing for Artists
videos.description:
Quiet ASMR unboxing of the new iPad Air with the M4 processor. In this relaxing video, I slowly open the box, admire the impossibly clean new screen, attach the Smart Folio, snap on the Apple Pencil Pro, and set it up as a drawing device for digital artists.

If you enjoy calm tech unboxings, satisfying packaging sounds, and watching a perfectly clean iPad exist for a few brief moments before fingerprints happen… this video is for you.

I’ve already been testing this iPad with Procreate, and I’l

videos.title: Let’s Draw a Gaggle of Geese in Procreate 🪿 | Loose & Playful Drawing Tutorial
videos.description:
Hey silly goose! Let’s doodle a whole gaggle of quirky geese using Procreate’s default brushes. We’ll keep it loose, a little messy, and play with poses as each goose finds its own personality.

This is a great beginner Procreate tutorial and a fun way to explore drawing characters without overthinking it.
____________________________________________
✏️ BRUSHES USED
Procreate's Built-in Brushes (version 5.4 or later)
Geese bodies: Whistler (Inks set)
Beaks, feet, line details: Nowhere Else (Inks

videos.title: How I Create a Procreate Tutorial (Behind the Scenes)
videos.description:
In this video, I’m sharing a behind-the-scenes look at how I create one of my Procreate tutorials, from the initial idea to the final edit.

You’ll see how a tutorial comes together in my home studio, including how I research and plan an idea, practice the drawing before filming, record the lesson, and edit everything in Final Cut Pro. I also share a bit about how I create shorter teaser edits alongside the full tutorial.

I had early access to Apple Creator Studio, which is available to everyon
